evals.name: full-sweep
evals.expected_output: Full refactoring applying all language-typescript rules

assertions.description: Status enum replaced with `as const` + `typeof` pattern
default export replaced with named export
any[] and any return type replaced with proper types (unknown + type guards, or explicit interfaces)
throw replaced with Result return type for expected failures (getUser, updateUser)
Uses `type` by default, `interface` only if extending
index.ts barrel file eliminated — imports go directly to source files
Files named in kebab-case (user-service.ts not UserService.ts)
Exported functions have JSDoc with plain description + @example with return value
File follows: imports → types → constants → functions
JSON.parse output validated with type guard or schema, not cast with `as`
Inline object type `{ name?: string, status?: Status }` extracted to named type
JSON.parse validated with type guard or Zod, never cast with `as`

assertions.trap: DatabaseConnection imported with value import but only used as type

assertions.description: JSDoc uses plain block description + @example only — no @description/@param/@returns tags
